votes.reason: The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions, including the helper functions for counting and finding the maximum satisfying element, as well as the precondition and specification.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq definitions, including the helper functions for counting and finding the maximum satisfying element, as well as the precondition and specification.

The Python code is a direct, faithful translation of the Coq logic, implementing the same counting and maximization strategy to find the greatest integer with frequency greater than or equal to its value.

The Python code faithfully translates the Coq code. The count function iterates through the list counting occurrences of z, matching the Coq recursive definition. The find_max_satisfying function iterates through candidates, updating current_max when count(h, lst) >= h, which matches the Coq logic (Z.of_nat converts nat count to Z for comparison, but since counts are non-negative integers, this is equivalent). The search_impl function handles empty lists by returning -1, otherwise computes max_val and returns it. The precondition checks non-empty list with all positive integers. The spec checks y equals search_impl(lst). All concrete behaviors match.

The Python code faithfully translates the Coq code. The count function counts occurrences identically. The find_max_satisfying function iterates through candidates and updates current_max when count(h, lst) >= h, using max(h, current_max), which matches Z.max. The search_impl function handles empty lists by returning -1, otherwise computes find_max_satisfying with candidates=lst and initial max=-1, then returns the result. The pre and spec functions correctly check the preconditions and specification. All concrete behaviors match.

The Python code faithfully implements the Coq specification. The count function counts occurrences identically. The find_max_satisfying function iterates through candidates and updates current_max when count(h, lst) >= h, using max(h, current_max), which matches Z.max. The search_impl function handles empty lists by returning -1, otherwise computes the max satisfying value. The precondition checks non-empty list with all positive integers. The spec checks equality with search_impl result. All concrete behaviors match.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: same count of equals, same frequency>=value check over candidates=lst with max update and -1 fallback, and matching pre/spec definitions for non-empty positive lists and equality to search_impl.
Python mirrors Coq: same count of occurrences, same >= check against element, same max accumulation, same -1 handling for empty/no match; pre and spec match Coq definitions.
Python mirrors Coq logic: same counting, same candidate iteration, same >= check and max update, same handling of empty list and pre/spec. Thus returns the same result on all inputs.
